WK-07.weekly_digest:
1
Compliance & Governance
 Achieved OPEX savings of 3.747M by shortening the ADTAN
project timeline from three years to two, aligned with the
copper sunset strategy.
Cloud HW Reclamation
 Presented the newly developed Cloud IT HW Reclamation
service in Tech-Portal on GM level with AGA/cloud services
GD.
Assets Registration
 Approved the BAT for the CR related to nominate CMDB as
single source of truth for stc servers. Next to deploy the new
service in production.
2 3
Disposal Governance
 Approved the BAT for Assets Disposal Dashboard with
AI&DS-files upload phase, next phase to start dashboards
development phase.
Satellite/ NTN
 Coordination on AST GWs D2D spectrum (ideally to be
similar to commercial) for POC to test interference on MW
links.
 OQ engagement has been initiated and a task force has
been established including all TU stakeholders
GSMA ISAG Voting
 Completed review and voting on key GSMA standards
updates impacting core and Open Gateway domains. This
engagement safeguarded stc’s architecture strategy and
strengthened our role in global standards governance.
4 5 6
Legacy Modernization and Migration
 Aligned with INFRA regarding LMM 3 proposed Strategy and
2026 action plan.
2G Shutdown
 Updated the 2G Shutdown challenges report to follow-up 2G
shutdown strategy execution.
TS – External Consultation
 Drove alignment with the Accenture team to initiate the
Technology Strategy – External Consultation project post-
Ramadan on 6-April, securing completion of core team
finalization by 18-March.
10 11 12
Completed
Abdullah H. F Alfaifi
Project Owner
Joining the investigation force led by Infrastructure sector and
to ensure GTU processes are being followed for product
development and PoC.
22 Jan 2026
 On Track
Sami H. Alzomaia
Project Owner
Develop a Proposal For Implementing Circular Economy
Principles Across All Subsidiaries, Starting With It Services
Offered By Solutions
30 June 2026
 Completed
Ahmed Alshaikh
Project Owner
Present to GCTO detailed status-quo on EA Repository and
show engagement model and support needed
26 Oct 2025
 Concluded the refreshment activity to generate an updated
cloudification recommendation list to be shared with ITP
team.
 Aligned with INFRA regarding ISP and FTTH Fundamental
Plans.
 Delivering public cloud tagging architecture as a mandatory
cost-governance foundation—enabling spend
transparency, accurate chargeback, and budget control to
avoid waste and weak accountability.
7 8 9
Cloudification Strategy Fixed Fundamental Plan Public Cloud Architecture
Tech-Refresh and Migration to public Cloud
 The Tech Refresh governance tracks (Process, ARB
Onboarding, and CCoE) have not yet formally initiated,
and no progress  recorded to date. As a result, the
governance, decision-making, and enablement project
of the Tech Refresh remains pending, which may impact
structured execution and alignment going forward.
Huawei IT Cloud proposal
 RFP has been received to support King Salman’s parks
requirements from EBU on the HCS IT Cloud and is
currently in the alignment phase with BA.
ERM Engagement
 A meeting was held with ERM and Excellence team to
agree on the procedure of rephrasing and transferring
the new risks. (10 Risks)
IA vacancies (FTEs, MSPs) Recruiting
 36 total CVs, 26 CVs shortlisted, 25 interviews done, 5
under Final Selection.
DR and Resiliency
 Resiliency guidelines document has been updated and
shared with management.
Databricks
 TCO analysis is ongoing to evaluate migrating ETL workloads
from Ab Initio and Teradata to Databricks.
SEA
 SEA tools assessment exercise is in closing stage (95%)
for both STC and MSP tools, updating the final reports in
progress.
CPP
 Agreed with Huawei on CCP Decommissioning to be
done in two different phases, each phase is expected to
take 6 months, no we're in the process of going through
the detail.
Reporting & Executive Visibility
 RFX automation; Executive (Monthly) Dashboard design
and reviews.
Enterprise Architecture (STAR)
 Architecture mapping and STAR integration are progressing, enhancing end-to-end visibility across applications and technology; additionally, two STAR awareness sessions were conducted
for the TSA team to strengthen adoption and alignment. Cybersecurity approvals and source data quality remain constraints.
 Key risks remain around data readiness and limited team capacity, with undefined access processes and resource constraints impacting overall acceleration post go-live.
Technology Governance & ARB
 Continued discussion and reviewed received inputs for S2R Controls maturity with TSA & TEC (awareness sessions and follow-up meetings with IA DA TS GDs; discussing draft inputs
received from TSBA-BA and  Architecture Governance)
 Follow-up with procurement on RfX & Procurement linkage and approach for different project types (e.g. MPA, projections) - awaiting their response.
 Successfully secured offline approvals from all Sectors and formally raised the process in APG.
GTU Blueprint 2026
Provided GTU feedback on the GSU watchlist meeting minutes presented at the C-level and aligned on the way forward for the 2026 GTU initiatives, with the following key highlights:
 The 2G Shutdown initiative will be removed unless the upcoming Telecom Committee decides to retain it and assigns the necessary prerequisites to the relevant BUs.
 CAPEX and OPEX savings targets for Fiber will be included once the GEP team provides the numbers; otherwise, these metrics will be omitted.
Technology Strategy
Architecture
Sector Weekly Report

Compliance
 Implemented an alternative solution in the Compliance Portal to update department names for STAR account cases.
 Raised 21 new STAR-related noncompliance cases across sectors (AIDS 8, CSDM 2, Infra 4, ITP 7) and converted 24 cases from Waived to Noncompliant following waiver expiry related to NGN
shutdown.
 Shared the first draft of the compliance assessment with HPG for validation and aligned with GTCO direction by coordinating with sectors and TSA to gather additional data and improve
visibility on open cases.

WK-08.gcto_updates:
Sector Weekly Report
Satellite/ NTN
 Deep dive with OQ proposed options analysis for NTN Nb
IoT connectivity to select the suitable options for stc
deployment.
ESM Optimization
 Share the Enhanced Subscription Model (ESM) IA optimization
proposal with TEC to be part of the consolidated proposal
presented to GCTO. The proposal aims to optimize costs
through detailed technical assessment and review of the
existing pricing mode
WK-08.weekly_digest:
1
Satellite/ NTN
 Deep dive with OQ proposed options analysis for NTN Nb-
IoT connectivity to select the suitable options for stc
deployment.
ESM Optimization
 Share the Enhanced Subscription Model (ESM) IA optimization
proposal with TEC to be part of the consolidated proposal
presented to GCTO. The proposal aims to optimize costs
through detailed technical assessment and review of the
existing pricing mode
Cost Optimization Opportunities
 As part of the Cloud and Core Architecture cost optimization
initiative with the Category Management team, we are
identifying structural cost optimization opportunities across
contracts, licensing, BoQ modeling, and infrastructure sizing,
enabling sustainable cloud cost reduction.
2 3
Cloud Roadmap
 IT cloud extensive RoadMap 2026-2029 has been shared with
Infra Planning team to be followed for stc 2026 deployment
plan.
GTU Dashboard
 Finalized GTU dashboard program cards for J2C and J2C
beyond programs in alignment with TEC PMO and including
sustainability and AI infra initiatives
Optical Networks Global Trends
 A technical workshop has been conducted with Nokia MEA
technical mangers to discuss the optics roadmap and global
trends for 2026. This workshop was a great opportunity to
discuss the new features and their relevance for stc in order
to update the architecture roadmap accordingly.
4 5 6
TSBA Playbook
 Revisiting the TS part in TSBA playbook to be clear and have
the needed details .
2G Shutdown
 Provided GTU feedback on the proposed GTU metrics and
milestones received from GSU. We are currently awaiting
the decision on the 2G shutdown, pending the outcome of
the Telecom Committee meeting.
Technology Strategy Contribution
 Contributed Technology Strategy insights during the
Strategy-to-Realization Value Chain design session on the
proposed process for Strategy Development.
10 11 12
On Track
Sami H. Alzomaia
Project Owner
Develop a Proposal For Implementing Circular Economy Principles Across All Subsidiaries, Starting With It Services Offered By Solutions
30 June 2026
 Site forecasting automation model has been validated and
the deployment automation phase has been initiated with
AIDS team.
 Successfully phased-out HCL Digital Experience and
replaced it with Adobe Experience Manager (Saving 4.7
MSAR)
 The IRAM milestones for the GTU blueprint will be deferred
to the end of Q1, in line with the feedback received from the
Head of the OSS Program.
7 8 9
Site Forecasting Using ML Technology Rationalization IRAM Milestones
Tech-Refresh and Migration to public Cloud
 A meeting with ITP team had been held, with
participation from IA, DA, AGA, and TSA VP Advisor. Key
points were discussed, and it was agreed to arrange
focused follow-up sessions to further discuss and
resolve outstanding items.
DR and Resiliency
 We have visited infrastructure VP and GMs to discuss
the Disaster Recovery (DR) Solution Architecture. Some
enhancements have been raised by infra aiming to
fortify the assessment framework and ensuring greater
resilience. The refined architecture will be presented for
final review and implementation.
ERM Engagement
 A meeting was held with ERM and Excellence team to
confirm the final IA list. (9 out of10 Risks were accepted
and one Risk transferred to DA).
IA vacancies (FTEs, MSPs) Recruiting
 Mobile Access consultants have been selected
Architecture Guild
 Held the sixth session of the Architecture Guild on DRA
components & Integration Framework.
Reporting & Executive Visibility
 Executive (Monthly) Dashboard design and reviews collating
TAG main functions
 ARB reporting automation in progress.
SEA
 Aligned with SEA on the roadmap of MSP tools.
Compliance
 Compliance & AI Enablement: Linked 150+ validated compliance cases with AI use-cases and aligned open items to the
Architecture Roadmap.
 Governance Progress: Closed 25 compliant cases and advanced HPG assessment on discovery agents and CMDB accuracy.
 GCTO Execution: ~60% of requested case details submitted; remaining to be finalized this week at stc.
Enterprise Architecture (STAR)
 Architecture mapping and STAR integration are progressing, enhancing end-to-end visibility across applications and technology; additionally, two STAR awareness sessions were conducted
for the TSA team to strengthen adoption and alignment. Cybersecurity approvals and source data quality remain constraints.
 Key risks remain around data readiness and limited team capacity, with undefined access processes and resource constraints impacting overall acceleration post go-live.
Technology Governance & ARB
 Continuing works and follow-ups on S2R Controls maturity: as-is; reviews with owners, and enhancements /action plan - with TEC and TSA
 CIAM HLD preliminary review completed; risk reduction guidance issued to CS,
 OSS NG NMS Consolidation HLD reviewed and feedback sent back to Stakeholders,
 Cloud & Core Standards fully reviewed and approved; elevated to Maturity Level 4 for publication
GTU Blueprint 2026
GCTO has been briefed about the 2026 Technology Unit (TU) which includes:
 GTU Blueprint KPIs – Fully aligned with GSU strategic objectives to ensure strategic coherence at the group level.
 Technology Unit KPIs – Covering the 2026 investment priorities and key focus areas to support execution of the technology roadmap
 Received feedback from CSDM on the GTU priorities 2026; following-up with the remaining Excellence GMs to obtain their input and complete the alignment process.
Technology Strategy
Architecture

 Assessment concluded with Cisco and SEA and approval
